differential_diagnoses: Head and neck malignancy (e.g., squamous cell carcinoma, lymphoma)
Vascular anomaly (e.g., pseudoaneurysm of the internal carotid or ascending pharyngeal artery)
Deep neck space infection with atypical pathogen
Pleomorphic adenoma of the minor salivary gland
next_steps: Emergent CT angiography (CTA) of the neck to rule out pseudoaneurysm before any further instrumentation
Tissue biopsy for histopathology and specialized staining (e.g., fungal, mycobacterial)
Laboratory workup for systemic autoimmune disease (e.g., ANCA, ACE, IgG4)
Consultation with interventional radiology for potential endovascular intervention if pseudoaneurysm is identified
Avoid further blind aspiration or incision until vascular pathology is definitively ruled out
